title: Meet N Greet

jokes.text: Someone will die. Of fun. And of murder.
jokes.setup: Andy's listing fun party elements in an innocent context
jokes.punchline: The dark turn to murder, then immediate pullback to fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy-style misdirection with excellent economy. The structure of setup-dark turn-immediate correction is expertly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We could have a bouncy castle, a dunk tank, maybe some face painting...

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We could murder someone!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I mean... we could have a murder mystery game! You know, like a fun party game where everyone tries to solve a fake murder.

jokes.text: Blood orphans. No blood orphans. I don't know what that is.
jokes.setup: Andy listing increasingly dark party elements
jokes.punchline: April's confused rejection of something that doesn't even make sense
jokes.explanation: Solid character work - Andy's nonsensical idea paired with April's deadpan correction. The absurdity fits their dynamic perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We could have a piñata, but instead of candy, it's full of hornets.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And instead of a stick, everyone just punches it with their bare fists.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Or we could just set the whole thing on fire and call it a bonfire.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Why would you set the piñata on fire? It's already full of hornets.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: That doesn't make any sense.

jokes.text: I really am amazing.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming everyone loves him for talking about himself constantly
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration of narcissistic self-promotion
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom character work - perfectly demonstrates his oblivious narcissism through immediate example.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Everyone loves me because I talk about myself constantly. It's my best quality.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Like, did you know I have a really good smile? People say it's my best feature.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Also, I'm an excellent listener. I love listening to people talk about me.

jokes.text: From Kernston Rubber Nipples. The Nipple King.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing important business contact
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of 'rubber nipples' and coronation as royalty
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist business name that becomes a running element. 'Nipple King' is perfectly quotable Parks and Rec absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'd like to introduce you to the Duke of Rubber Nipples, sovereign ruler of the Kingdom of Inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Ted Flirtman or Rex Baggs... I made up both those names.
jokes.setup: Tom testing Leslie's knowledge of Kernston's advisors
jokes.punchline: Revealing he fabricated the elaborate test
jokes.explanation: Well-structured gotcha moment that reveals Tom's consulting style while catching Leslie in over-preparation.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, Leslie, I'm gonna test your knowledge of Kernston's advisors. Who was Kernston's trade minister in 1987?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Um... Gerald Hutchins?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wrong. It was actually... I have no idea. I made up this entire test. Kernston isn't even a real place.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I just wanted to see how long you'd pretend to know about something you didn't actually know about.

jokes.text: I'm a sumo wrestler after he lost the weight.
jokes.setup: Context of Andy in an ill-fitting costume
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic of a deflated sumo wrestler costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with total confidence. The visual absurdity enhances the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I look like a sumo wrestler that got deflated.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Which is weird because normally you'd think a deflated sumo wrestler would be skinnier, not... worse.

jokes.text: My parents' method of problem solving is to kind of keep everything bottled up and just subtly hint at what's bothering them. And after 36 years, they are still divorced.
jokes.setup: Ben describing his family's non-confrontational approach
jokes.punchline: The deadpan revelation that this method spectacularly failed
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery that sets up Ben's passive-aggressive behavior while being genuinely funny about family dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My family never argued. We just... didn't talk about problems.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My dad left when I was seven and we all just pretended he was at work for ten years.

jokes.text: It's always been a dream of mine to be a rug, and it's finally happened.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking why Tom's face is on rugs at her political event
jokes.punchline: Tom's completely sincere fulfillment of bizarre life goal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist character work. The sincerity of Tom achieving his 'dream' of being a rug is perfectly ridiculous and uniquely Tom.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, why is your face on all these rugs?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Because it's one of my life goals to have my face on a rug. I've achieved it.

jokes.text: You are a policewoman. Yep. You're a regular... Sherlock Holmes. I solved that mystery before you did.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to guess Donna's obvious costume
jokes.punchline: Donna's smug claim of superior detective work for the obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Chris's enthusiastic obviousness paired with Donna's confident superiority complex.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Donna, what are you dressed as?

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: I'm a detective.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: How can you tell?

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Because I'm extremely observant.

jokes.text: Replace his pasty British frailty with superhuman physical fitness, and you get... Sherlock Traeger.
jokes.setup: Chris describing Sherlock Holmes as the greatest character
jokes.punchline: His narcissistic conclusion that he's an improved version
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger character work - his relentless positivity and self-improvement obsession leading to narcissistic conclusions.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Sherlock Holmes is the greatest character ever created. A man of unparalleled intellect and deductive reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: But honestly, I think I'm a more well-rounded version of him. I have his analytical mind, but I also have emotional intelligence and the ability to connect with people on a human level.

jokes.text: You can text without looking at your phone? I think it's rude not to maintain eye contact with the people that I'm talking to.
jokes.setup: Jerry noticing Chris texting without looking
jokes.punchline: Chris's backwards logic that rudeness prevention enables the impossible skill
jokes.explanation: Great Chris character moment - his impossible standards and politeness creating an absurd superhuman ability.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Chris, you're texting without looking at your phone.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I know. I'm very polite. I make eye contact with people when I talk to them. So I've developed the ability to text without looking.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's not how that works.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Actually, it is. I'm living proof.

jokes.text: Shock wire. I call it that, 'cause if you take a shower, and you touch the wire, you die!
jokes.setup: Ron pointing out dangerous exposed wire above bathtub
jokes.punchline: Andy's cheerful naming convention for a deadly hazard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character work - his childlike enthusiasm for naming a potentially lethal household hazard.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Andy, there's a live wire hanging directly above the bathtub.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh, I call that the Shock Tub 3000.

jokes.text: A hammer, half of a pretzel, baseball card, some cartridge that says Sonic and Hedgehog, a scissor half, and a flashlight. Filled with jelly beans.
jokes.setup: Ron opening April and Andy's toolbox expecting tools
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd non-tool items culminating in candy-filled flashlight
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that perfectly captures Andy and April's chaotic lifestyle through Ron's deadpan delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: What is all this? I expected tools, not... is this a unicorn plushie?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: It's a mood ring unicorn, Ron.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: And what's this? A bottle of nail polish labeled 'Sunset Dreams'?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's for when we need a manicure break during woodworking.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: A tiara. There's a tiara in here.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: For formal tool use.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: And finally, a flashlight filled with... candy?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So you can see AND snack in the dark, Ron.

jokes.text: That's because I don't hate myself, Tania.
jokes.setup: Business owner challenging Leslie about never buying salad
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt, unapologetic anti-salad stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope moment - her unwavering commitment to unhealthy food preferences even when politically disadvantageous.

dialogue.speaker: Business Owner
dialogue.line: You've never bought a salad from us.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's true. I don't eat salad. I think salad is what food eats.

jokes.text: The details. Most people would probably say the deets. I say the 'tails. Just one example of innovation.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to impress Martin Kernston with business talk
jokes.punchline: His proud presentation of pointless linguistic 'innovation'
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Tom character work - his tendency to rebrand common things as innovation perfectly captured.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Mr. Kernston, I'd like to present my new business concept: 'Rent-tainment.' It's like rent, but it's also entertainment.

dialogue.speaker: Martin Kernston
dialogue.line: That's just rent.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Rent-tainment. The 'tainment' is silent.

jokes.text: Look, I don't like to throw around the word butt-head too often. If you call everybody a butt-head, then it kind of loses its impact. But I can say without hesitation that Tom is being a real dick.
jokes.setup: Leslie's measured approach to using 'butt-head'
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to much harsher language
jokes.explanation: Great structure - the careful consideration of 'butt-head' usage followed by immediate deployment of stronger language.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I need to be very careful about my language. I can't just go around calling people 'butt-head' willy-nilly.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's why I have a carefully curated list of insults I use only in the most extreme circumstances.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Like... buttface, turd-guzzling thunder-cunt, and worse.

jokes.text: You, bean bag, come with me. I'm an eggplant. I don't care. Come with me.
jokes.setup: Ron needing help with home repair project
jokes.punchline: His complete disregard for Jerry's costume correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - his practical focus overriding social niceties, plus treating Jerry like an object.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Jerry, I need your help with a home repair project.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh, Ron, I'd be happy to help! By the way, it's not a costume, it's my outfit.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't care what you call it, Gerry. Now hand me that wrench.

jokes.text: He's going as lame.
jokes.setup: Andy asking where Ben's costume is
jokes.punchline: April's cutting assessment of Ben's non-participation
jokes.explanation: Classic April deadpan delivery with perfect economy. The insult is brief but effective.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: He's not doing it. He said it was 'undignified.' Which is hilarious, because Ben once spent an entire meeting with the state comptroller with a Cheeto stuck to his forehead.

jokes.text: My name's Ben. I'm mad.
jokes.setup: Andy pressuring Ben to express his feelings
jokes.punchline: Ben's robotic, minimalist emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character moment - his uncomfortable relationship with emotional expression delivered with maximum economy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Come on, Ben. Just tell me how you really feel.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I feel... adequate.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Adequate? That's it?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My heart rate is normal. I have consumed sufficient calories. These are positive indicators.

jokes.text: Enormous Kenny's Fried Dough Stand and Mobile Phone Emporium.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing local businesses at the event
jokes.punchline: The absurd combination of fried dough and mobile phones
jokes.explanation: Good Parks and Rec absurd business name that feels authentically small-town bizarre.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We've got Jerry's Fried Dough, Sweetums, and the Mobile Phone Emporium all here tonight!

jokes.text: You're lucky that Martin Kernston is here, 'cause you're gonna need another nipple.
jokes.setup: Leslie threatening Tom for hijacking her event
jokes.punchline: Threatening to harm him specifically in Kernston's area of expertise
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that creates a specific, absurd threat tied to the episode's running element.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, if you hijack this event, I will hurt you in a way that only Ben Wyatt could describe using his forensic accounting skills.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, what?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm going to make your finances so complicated that even the IRS won't understand them.

jokes.text: Damn it, I just love it so much.
jokes.setup: Ben experiencing satisfaction from successful home repair
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming emotional response to manual competency
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ben's surprise at enjoying practical work, contrasting with his usual bureaucratic nature.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I fixed the shelf. I actually fixed it. Do you understand what this means? I'm a man now. A real man. I can build things. I can provide. I could survive in the wilderness.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You used a level and some screws.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I am a god.

jokes.text: I invented Facebook.
jokes.setup: Mysterious testimonial about Entertainment 720's success
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous claim to have invented Facebook
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment showing Tom's fake testimonials, though feels slightly cheap using real celebrity reference.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Entertainment 720 is the best company ever. We invented Facebook.

jokes.text: I have one sister. We steal each other's stuff, hack each other's Twitter accounts, set each other's clothes on fire. There are no rules.
jokes.setup: April describing sibling relationship
jokes.punchline: Escalation to literal arson as normal sibling behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that perfectly captures April's dark humor and explains her aggressive approach to conflict.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: My brother and I have a very normal sibling relationship. We fight, we make up, we set each other's stuff on fire. You know, typical brother and sister stuff.

jokes.text: The Dwyer method, which was yelling, wrestling, crying followed by lots of hugs. And then more wrestling, but the fun kind. And then crying when the fun kind of wrestling got out of hand.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his family's conflict resolution
jokes.punchline: The cyclical nature of violence and reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character background that explains Andy's physical approach to problems while being genuinely funny about family dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My family has this thing where we fight, then we make up, then we fight again. It's like a cycle.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My dad would punch my uncle, then they'd go get a beer together, then next Thanksgiving my uncle would punch my dad back.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's beautiful, really. Very balanced.

jokes.text: My company is bankrupt, okay? Entertainment 720 is dead.
jokes.setup: Tom's bizarre behavior and defensive attitude all episode
jokes.punchline: The reveal that his company has completely failed
jokes.explanation: Important character moment that recontextualizes Tom's behavior, though more dramatic than comedic.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Look, everything is fine. My company is doing great. We're expanding, we're profitable, we're—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, your office is literally a closet.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It's a small space, that's part of our brand. We're lean, we're efficient—

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Your website just says 'Tom's Company' and then it's a 404 error.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, well, we're still in beta. The company has completely failed.

jokes.text: I don't know. I guess I just didn't moisturize enough this morning... With the company, Tom.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking what happened to the company
jokes.punchline: Tom's oblivious focus on his skin in the hot tub
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's tendency toward superficial concerns even in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What happened to the company?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh, it dissolved. But look at my skin right now — I'm like a soft, buttery biscuit.

jokes.text: Psych! Blood capsules.
jokes.setup: Everyone believing Andy's nose is actually broken
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the blood was fake all along
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening scene that reframes the entire conflict resolution sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My nose is broken! There's blood everywhere!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh no, Andy, we need to get you to a hospital!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: This is so serious. Everyone, we need to help Andy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Actually, it's fake blood. I was just messing with you guys.

jokes.text: Well, hindsight is 20/20. Kind of seems like regular sight should have caught that one.
jokes.setup: Tom defending his business strategy with a cliché
jokes.punchline: Leslie pointing out the obviousness of the mistake
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that cleverly deconstructs a common saying while delivering a sharp insult.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You've gotta spend money to make money, Leslie. It's just good business sense.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, that's not a business strategy, that's just... math. And also the reason your last three ventures failed.

jokes.text: Stop using my comforter for your pillow forts... It's going to be about 5,600 bucks. Deal?
jokes.setup: Ben laying out reasonable roommate requests
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely disproportionate counter-demand
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's complete misunderstanding of negotiation and compromise.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Look, I just have a few reasonable roommate requests. No loud music after 10 PM, please clean up after yourself, and we should probably establish a chore schedule.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. I hear you. But here's my counter-offer: I get to have a pet ocelot, I eat whatever I want from the fridge without asking, and on Thursdays I get to invite the entire Parks Department over to watch me practice my DJ skills until 4 AM.

jokes.text: Peter Gabriel leaves Genesis.
jokes.setup: Tom listing troubles of 1975 alongside Watergate and Vietnam
jokes.punchline: Treating a band member departure as equally traumatic
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor placing pop culture events alongside major historical tragedies.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: In 1975, we had Watergate, we had Vietnam, and we had to say goodbye to David Bowie's Ziggy Stardust persona.

jokes.text: Tom Haverford is a selfish, unctuous, sleazy, self-promoting, good-hearted, secretly kind and wonderful, tiny little person.
jokes.setup: Leslie apparently insulting Tom in her testimonial
jokes.punchline: The turn to genuine affection while ending with 'tiny little person'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's complex relationship with Tom - genuine affection mixed with honest assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom is a lot of things. He's lazy, he's vain, he's a terrible friend.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: But what Tom is, above all else, is a tiny little person. And I mean that with all the love in my heart.

jokes.text: And we may be having intercourse. Please, you've got to stop saying things like that to me.
jokes.setup: Chris being overly honest with Jerry about dating his daughter
jokes.punchline: Jerry's desperate plea to stop the TMI
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris character work - his inappropriate honesty creating maximum discomfort for Jerry.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Jerry, I want to be completely transparent with you. I'm seeing your daughter, and I think it's important you know that our physical relationship is very—

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Chris, please, I'm begging you. I do not need to know this. I really, really don't.

jokes.text: Solve this mystery, genius.
jokes.setup: Chris being called a detective earlier
jokes.punchline: April's sarcastic challenge about his lost keys
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback that deflates Chris's detective enthusiasm with mundane reality.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Oh wow, a detective. So where are your keys?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Because I've been watching you pat down your pockets for twenty minutes.

tmdb_overview: Andy and April host a Halloween bash at their house. Tom emcees an event for Leslie's campaign. Andy and April receive an unusual wedding present from Ron and Ann.
insight: Character comedy carries a narrowly-missed top-tier episode with 34 jokes in 49 minutes.
